actions.description: Lookup a specific Asteroid based on its NASA JPL small body (SPK-ID) ID.
actions.name: getAsteroid

properties.description: The unique identifier of the asteroid.
properties.name: id

properties.description: NASA's Near Earth Object reference ID for the asteroid.
properties.name: neo_reference_id

properties.description: The official name or designation of the asteroid.
properties.name: name

properties.description: A URL to the NASA JPL page with detailed information about the asteroid.
properties.name: nasa_jpl_url

properties.description: The absolute magnitude of the asteroid, representing its intrinsic brightness.
properties.name: absolute_magnitude_h

properties.description: Indicates whether the asteroid is considered potentially hazardous to Earth.
properties.name: is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid

properties.description: Estimated diameter measurements of the asteroid in various units.
properties.name: estimated_diameter

properties.description: Estimated diameter range expressed in kilometers.
properties.name: kilometers

properties.description: The minimum estimated diameter of the asteroid in kilometers.
properties.name: estimated_diameter_min

properties.description: The maximum estimated diameter of the asteroid in kilometers.
properties.name: estimated_diameter_max

properties.description: A list of records describing close approaches of the asteroid to celestial bodies.

items.description: A list of records describing close approaches of the asteroid to celestial bodies.

properties.description: The date when the asteroid makes its close approach.
properties.name: close_approach_date

properties.description: The velocity of the asteroid relative to the body it approaches.
properties.name: relative_velocity

properties.description: The relative velocity in kilometers per second.
properties.name: kilometers_per_second

properties.description: The relative velocity in kilometers per hour.
properties.name: kilometers_per_hour

properties.description: The distance by which the asteroid misses the celestial body.
properties.name: miss_distance

properties.description: The miss distance measured in kilometers.
properties.name: kilometers

properties.description: The miss distance expressed in lunar distances (distance from Earth to Moon).
properties.name: lunar

properties.description: The celestial body that the asteroid is approaching (e.g., Earth).
properties.name: orbiting_body
close_approach_data

properties.description: Orbital parameters describing the asteroid's trajectory.
properties.name: orbital_data

properties.description: A unique identifier for the asteroid's orbit solution.
properties.name: orbit_id

properties.description: A measure of how much the orbit deviates from a perfect circle.
properties.name: eccentricity

properties.description: The semi-major axis of the asteroid's orbit, representing its average distance from the Sun.
properties.name: semi_major_axis

properties.description: The tilt of the asteroid's orbit relative to the plane of the solar system.
properties.name: inclination

properties.description: The time it takes for the asteroid to complete one full orbit around the Sun.
properties.name: orbital_period

actions.title: Get Asteroid

actions.description: Returns NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.
actions.name: getPictureOfTheDay

properties.description: The date of the Astronomy Picture of the Day.
properties.name: date

properties.description: A detailed explanation of the image or video provided by NASA.
properties.name: explanation

properties.description: The type of media returned (e.g., 'image' or 'video').
properties.name: media_type

properties.description: The version of the NASA API service used to generate this response.
properties.name: service_version

properties.description: The title of the Astronomy Picture of the Day.
properties.name: title

properties.description: The URL where the standard resolution image or video can be accessed.
properties.name: url

properties.description: The URL for the high-definition version of the image, if available.
properties.name: hdurl

properties.description: The date of the Astronomy Picture of the Day.
properties.name: date

properties.description: A detailed explanation of the image or video provided by NASA.
properties.name: explanation

properties.description: Select how you want to fetch the picture of the day.
properties.label: Fetch Method
properties.name: queryType

properties.description: The date of the APOD image to retrieve.
properties.label: Date
properties.name: date

properties.description: The start of a date range.
properties.label: Start Date
properties.name: start_date

properties.description: The end of the date range.
properties.label: End Date
properties.name: end_date

properties.description: If specified, returns a randomly chosen images.
properties.label: Count
properties.name: count

properties.description: Return the URL of video thumbnail.
properties.label: Thumbs
properties.name: thumbs

actions.title: Get Astronomy Picture of the Day

description: NASA is a United States government agency that provides public APIs offering access to space-related data, including astronomy images, Mars rover photos, and other scientific information for developers and applications.
name: nasa
title: NASA
